  "findings": [
    "Reverse-lapped water-resistive barrier (WRB) with the upper layer tucked behind the lower layer, creating a water-shedding failure."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop siding installation immediately.",
    "Re-lap the WRB layers so the upper layer overlaps the lower layer by a minimum of 2 inches to ensure proper shingle-fashion drainage."
  ],
  "rationale": "The WRB is installed in a reverse-lap configuration, which violates the fundamental principle of gravity-shedding water management. Water running down the upper layer will be directed behind the lower layer and onto the sheathing, leading to rot and structural damage. This must be corrected before any exterior cladding or siding is installed over it.",
  "references": [
    "IRC Section R703.1.1",
    "IRC Section R703.2",
    "Manufacturer installation instructions for WRB"
  ]
}
